description.en-US: A short treatise composed for James of Tonengo on the moral status of casting lots — when (if ever) it is permissible and what the practice signifies.
description.la: Tractatus brevis Iacobo de Tonengo compositus, de statu morali sortium — quando liceant et quid significent.

sources.description: English translation drawn from the Geremia/AquinasOperaOmnia GitHub repository.
Latin Leonine / Marietti edition (public domain), mirrored from dhspriory.org via the Geremia/AquinasOperaOmnia GitHub repository.

title.en-US: Chapter 0 — Foreword — [Prologue, which declares the author's intention]
title.la: Capitulum 0 — Foreword — [Prologue, which declares the author's intention]

title.en-US: Chapter 1 — The matters into which a search for information by lots can be made
title.la: Capitulum 1 — The matters into which a search for information by lots can be made

title.en-US: Chapter 2 — The end to which lots are ordered is disclosed
title.la: Capitulum 2 — The end to which lots are ordered is disclosed

title.en-US: Chapter 3 — The manner of seeking information through lots
title.la: Capitulum 3 — The manner of seeking information through lots

title.en-US: Chapter 4 — Whence lots derive their power
title.la: Capitulum 4 — Whence lots derive their power

title.en-US: Chapter 5 — Whether it is permitted to use lots
title.la: Capitulum 5 — Whether it is permitted to use lots
